response:
Sarah owns a small retail shop that supports local factory workers by selling their handmade products. She decides to run a promotional week where she offers a 15% discount on all items produced by the factory workers. If a customer purchases a handcrafted table originally priced at $200 and a set of factory-made chairs originally priced at $150, how much does the customer pay for these items during the promotional week? Remember to apply the discount to each item separately.

Reach out to the local community to spread the word about the promotional week. The yard sale event will be held in the town square, and it will feature a variety of handmade items, including the table and chairs that are currently on sale. The discount on these items makes them more accessible to customers who are looking for quality products at a reasonable price. Milk is a common item found in many households, and it's often used in cooking or as a drink. The promotional week is a great opportunity for customers to find unique items while also supporting local artisans.
